reasoning: The ECB deposit facility rate was 2.00% as of the knowledge cutoff (2025-11-09), having been held steady at the October 30, 2025 meeting. Professional forecaster consensus (Reuters poll) and market pricing as of late October 2025 indicate that the ECB is expected to maintain this 2.00% rate until at least 2027, with inflation projected to fall to 1.7% in 2026 (below the 2% target). There is virtually no narrative or economic pressure for a rate hike. For the first two resolution dates (Nov 16 and Dec 9), no monetary policy meetings are scheduled, making a change extremely unlikely. The third date (Feb 7) follows a January 2026 meeting, but given the inflation and growth outlook, a hike remains highly improbable. Uncertainty is increased slightly for the more distant date.
